subject: Ecology
title: Deep-Sea Gigantism
description: A fast breakdown of Deep-Sea Gigantism for builders.

hook.text: Giant squid, six-foot isopods, and spider crabs—deep-sea creatures grow strangely large.

buildup.text: Scientists call it deep-sea gigantism, but nobody fully agrees on why it happens.

discovery.text: Cold temperatures slow metabolism, letting organisms grow for decades before reproducing.

twist.text: High pressure may also play a role, altering cell chemistry in ways we barely understand.

climax.text: Giant isopods can survive over five years without eating—size helps them store reserves.

punchline.text: The deep sea grows things slowly and strangely.

quiz.question: What may contribute to deep-sea gigantism?
